code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a comics character into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    # Mapping of predicates to natural‑language phrase templates
    templates = {
        "alternativeName": "{subject} is also known as {object}",
        "creator": "{subject} was created by {object}",
        "fullName": "the full name of {subject} is {object}",
        "foundedBy": "{subject} was founded by {object}",
        "keyPerson": "the key person at {subject} is {object}",
        "city": "{subject} is located in {object}",
        "product": "{subject} produces {object}",
        "lastAired": "the last episode aired on {object}",
        "starring": "{subject} stars {object}",
        "firstAppearanceInFilm": "{subject} first appeared in the film {object}",
        "voice": "the voice of {subject} is provided by {object}",
        "distributor": "{subject} was distributed by {object}",
        "birthPlace": "{subject} was born in {object}",
        "nationality": "{subject} is {object}",
        "award": "{subject} received the {object}",
        "broadcastedBy": "{subject} was broadcasted by {object}",
        "firstAired": "{subject} first aired on {object}",
        "series": "{subject} is part of the series {object}",
        "child": "{subject} has a child named {object}",
    }

    phrases = []
    for triple in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(triple.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            # Preserve quotes in object if they exist
            obj = triple.object
            # Strip surrounding quotes for readability but keep them if they are part of a title
            if (obj.startswith('"') and obj.endswith('"')) or (obj.startswith("'") and obj.endswith("'")):
                obj = obj[1:-1]
            phrase = tmpl.format(subject=triple.subject, object=obj)
            phrases.append(phrase)

    if not phrases:
        return ""

    # Join phrases into a single sentence
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        sentence = phrases[0].capitalize() + "."
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(phrases[:-1])
        sentence += ", and " + phrases[-1] + "."
        sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
